pitch.title: Five Seconds to Midnight
pitch.logline: In a neon-drenched metropolis, a cynical detective who can see five seconds into the future must protect a mysterious witness from a syndicate of time-manipulating assassins.
pitch.synopsis:
Act 1: Detective Jax uses his 5-second foresight to win bar fights and scrape by as a low-rent PI in Neo-Angeles. He is hired to find a missing woman, only to witness her murder by an assassin who seems to anticipate his every move.

Act 2: Jax discovers the assassin belongs to a cartel trading in chronal-tech. He finds the victim's sister, who holds the key to a device that can alter the past. As they go on the run, Jax must stretch his 5-second limit to its breaking point to outmaneuver heavily armed precog hitmen.

Act 3: In a mind-bending shootout at the cartel's headquarters, Jax realizes his 5-second advantage isn't just observation, but the ability to choose alternate timelines. He sacrifices his own future to save the girl and dismantle the syndicate.

act_1.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence 1: The Hustle and the Hit

beats.description: Detective Jax navigates the neon-drenched slums of Neo-Angeles. He uses his five-second foresight to effortlessly win a brutal bar fight and collect a meager bounty, establishing his cynical worldview and reliance on his unique ability to scrape by.

A wealthy client hires Jax to find a missing woman. Jax tracks her to an abandoned warehouse, but before he can secure her, a mysterious assassin attacks. Jax uses his foresight, but the assassin anticipates his every counter-move and murders the woman, leaving Jax stunned and defeated.

act_1.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence 2: The Sister and the Syndicate

beats.description: Realizing his five-second advantage was countered, Jax investigates the assassin's origins. He discovers a shadowy cartel trading in illegal chronal-tech. Therefore, he digs into the dead woman's past and learns she was trying to protect her younger sister, Elara.

Jax locates Elara just as the cartel's precog hitmen arrive to silence her. Recognizing she holds the key to the cartel's motives, Jax makes a split-second decision to intervene. They barely escape the ambush, locking Jax into a deadly conflict and forcing them on the run.

act_2.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence 3: Outmatched and on the Run

beats.description: Jax and Elara navigate the treacherous underbelly of Neo-Angeles. Elara reveals she possesses a biometric key to a prototype device capable of altering the past, which the cartel desperately wants.

The cartel corners them in a crowded mag-train station. Jax must stretch his five-second limit to its absolute breaking point to predict multiple heavily armed precog hitmen simultaneously. He suffers severe physical toll but manages to derail the train cars to secure their escape.

Hiding in a safehouse, Jax and Elara decode the biometric key. They learn the cartel intends to use the device to erase their rivals from history entirely, establishing an unstoppable monopoly on time itself.

Jax decides they cannot just run; they must steal the device before the cartel activates it. They infiltrate a heavily guarded chronal-tech facility. In a massive, time-distorted set-piece, Jax shifts from a reactive survivor to a proactive hero, successfully securing the device but alerting the cartel boss to their exact location.

With the device in hand, Jax and Elara attempt to find a way to destroy it safely. During a quiet moment, Jax bonds with Elara, realizing he finally has something worth fighting for beyond his next paycheck. He begins experimenting with his power, trying to push past the five-second barrier.

The cartel boss initiates a city-wide temporal lockdown, trapping Jax and Elara in a quarantined sector. Jax attempts to fight their way out, but the precog assassins use coordinated chronal-grenades to disrupt his visions, severely wounding him.
Bleeding and exhausted, Jax tries to lead Elara to the sector borders. However, the cartel boss uses a larger chronal-engine to predict Jax's exact escape route, setting an inescapable trap.

The cartel ambushes them in an abandoned chronal-reactor core. Jax is overwhelmed and brutally beaten. The boss captures Elara, reclaims the device, and leaves Jax for dead as the facility is rigged to explode. Jax has lost everything.

Surviving the explosion by a fraction of a second, a broken Jax experiences a massive temporal feedback loop. He realizes his five-second advantage is not merely observation, but the ability to actively choose and collapse alternate timelines. He is not predicting the future; he is writing it.

Armed with this mind-bending revelation, Jax storms the cartel headquarters. He moves like a ghost, intentionally collapsing failed timelines to perfectly dodge bullets and dismantle the syndicate's elite guards, breaching the inner sanctum.

Jax confronts the cartel boss in the temporal chamber just as the device is activated. A surreal shootout occurs across multiple overlapping timelines. Jax realizes the only way to save Elara and destroy the machine is to lock in a timeline where he takes the fatal shot meant for her.

Jax sacrifices himself, collapsing the timelines into a single reality where the device overloads and kills the cartel boss. In the aftermath, a safe Elara walks through a brighter, cartel-free Neo-Angeles, honoring the cynical detective who gave up his own future to ensure hers.

characters.name: Detective Jax
characters.brief_summary: A cynical, low-rent private investigator in Neo-Angeles who uses his five-second foresight to win bar fights and scrape by. He operates under the assumption that the future is fixed and nothing matters, until he is forced to protect a witness who challenges his worldview.

psychological_core.wound: He previously foresaw a tragic accident involving a loved one but was unable to change the outcome, convincing him his power is a curse.
psychological_core.false_belief: The universe is entirely deterministic, meaning free will is an illusion and trying to change the world is pointless.
psychological_core.fear: Being responsible for someone else's life and failing them all over again.
psychological_core.desire: To make enough credits to numb himself and survive another day without getting emotionally involved.
psychological_core.need: To realize he has the agency to write the future, embracing free will even if it costs him his life.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Terse, predictive, and impatient. He often interrupts people right before they finish their sentence and NEVER talks about his past or expresses overt optimism.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: He points out an immediate, mundane physical detail in the room or anticipates a minor physical inconvenience, like catching a falling glass, to abruptly change the subject.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Jax shouldn't act like a magical wizard; he just seems like a guy with incredibly fast reflexes and a slightly bored demeanor. His habit of finishing people's sentences should feel like natural conversational overlap rather than psychic parlor tricks, only becoming obvious and intense when he is deeply stressed.

characters.name: Silas Thorne (Cartel Boss)
characters.brief_summary: A ruthless, calculating syndicate leader who has monopolized chronal-tech to stay ten steps ahead of his enemies. He views time as a commodity to be hoarded and seeks to erase his rivals from existence to achieve absolute control.

psychological_core.wound: A profound past betrayal that made him realize the chaotic, unpredictable nature of human free will is a fatal threat.
psychological_core.false_belief: Total control over time is the only way to achieve perfect order and absolute security.
psychological_core.fear: The unknown, and the possibility of a variable or timeline he cannot predict or control.
psychological_core.desire: To activate the chronal-engine and rewrite history to cement his eternal, unchallenged rule.
psychological_core.need: To accept that time and human will cannot be perfectly subjugated, a lesson he learns too late.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Measured, perfectly paced, and eerily calm. He NEVER raises his voice, rushes his words, or uses filler words like um or uh.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: He answers a probing question by revealing a disturbing piece of the questioner's own immediate future, weaponizing his precog intel to unnerve them.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Silas behaves like a bored CEO who has already read the minutes of the meeting rather than a cartoon villain. He rarely makes eye contact because he is often looking at invisible temporal overlays, making him seem aloof and detached rather than overtly menacing until his plans are genuinely threatened.

characters.name: Elara
characters.brief_summary: A resilient survivor whose biometric signature is the key to a reality-altering prototype device. Thrust into a deadly conspiracy after her sister's murder, she serves as the moral anchor who forces Jax to care about the future.

psychological_core.wound: The sudden, brutal murder of her older sister, who was her only remaining family and protector.
psychological_core.false_belief: She is a helpless liability who brings death and misfortune to anyone trying to protect her.
psychological_core.fear: That her sister died for nothing and the cartel will use her existence to rewrite history for the worse.
psychological_core.desire: To destroy the chronal device and ensure the cartel can never hurt anyone else.
psychological_core.need: To step out of her sister's shadow, stop running, and take active control of her own destiny.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Earnest, question-heavy, and slightly breathless when anxious. She NEVER uses fatalistic language or accepts defeat.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: She hyper-focuses on the technical aspects of the chronal-tech or the immediate logistics of their escape rather than addressing her grief.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Elara masks her deep survivor's guilt with utility, constantly trying to make herself useful by checking ammo or decoding tech so she doesn't have to sit with her feelings. She doesn't weep openly; her grief only leaks out in rare moments of stillness when she thinks no one is looking.
